key_insight: A physics-constrained graph-based calibration framework can identify stable lumped thermal models for spacecraft directly from sparse temperature trajectories and inputs.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, physically admissible thermal digital twins that can support spacecraft design, monitoring, and long-horizon prediction without detailed material or geometry-based parameterization.
method: Formulate LPTM identification as a trajectory-matching inverse problem on a graph dynamical system.
Enforce physics by construction through positive nodal coefficients and symmetric conductive couplings.
Calibrate the reduced model from temperature measurements and known inputs, avoiding ad hoc regularization.
evidence_signal: The approach is validated on synthetic data from high-fidelity finite element simulations, including progressively more complex forcing, and it reproduces long-term temperature evolution robustly under measurement noise.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on synthetic FE-generated datasets, so transfer to real spacecraft telemetry will depend on sensor placement, excitation richness, and model mismatch.
paper_title: Physics-constrained identification of graph-based thermal networks for spacecraft digital twins
